mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.0% signals labor market cooling that reduces corporate earnings growth expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5d returns are positive in >60% of instances when UNRATE crosses 4.0%, or t-stat of return difference vs baseline is insignificant at p>0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad consumer demand contraction, which reduces corporate revenue forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60d returns with a logit coefficient significant at p<0.05, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2% year-over-year reflects robust supply-side capacity utilization that supports corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth >2% correlates with negative 20d returns in >58% of observations during recovery regimes, or rolling IC turns negative for 12 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with rapid federal funds rate hikes forces aggressive portfolio de-risking, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS changes shows no predictive power for 5d returns (p>0.10), or positive returns dominate when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate consumer spending power, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 60d returns with a statistically significant coefficient, or the signal Sharpe ratio falls below 0.5 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth dropping below 1.0% signals economic stagnation that compresses equity risk premiums, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain positive in >62% of quarters where GDPC1 growth is under 1.0%, or the predictive log-likelihood ratio is insignificant at p>0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates supply chain bottlenecks easing while labor remains tight, creating a positive earnings surprise that boosts S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and falling UNRATE yields negative 5d returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample validation.
